title: Roe v. Wade
description: The Supreme Court invents a 'right to privacy' that encompasses the right to kill a child. This unconstitutional decree stripped personhood from the pre-born.

title: Doe v. Bolton
description: Released alongside Roe, this Georgia case defined 'health of the mother' so broadly that abortion was effectively allowed on demand through all nine months.

title: Planned Parenthood v. Casey
description: The Court upheld Roe and established the 'Undue Burden' standard, ensuring abortion remained protected and allowing states only minimal regulations.

title: Georgia's Heartbeat Bill (HB 481)
description: Georgia bans abortion after a detectable heartbeat. Still allows abortion before 6 weeks. Exceptions remain. Children before this threshold have no protection.

title: Dobbs v. Jackson
description: Roe v. Wade overturned. Authority to protect life returns to the states. The excuse of 'federal tyranny' is removed.

title: HB 441 Introduced
description: Georgia Equal Protection Act introduced. Life from fertilization. No exceptions. Speaker Burns killed it in committee—April 2026.
